hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown over the same 20% window (same integral, C¹-smooth transitions — avoids the derivative kink at warmdown entry and softens the LR approach to zero through the late-QAT flip)
notes: Only change: lr_mul() in train loop. Stable phase identical; within warmdown window compute x=(frac-(1-wd))/wd and return 0.5*(1+cos(pi*x)). Integral 0.5*warmdown_frac matches linear so baseline peak LRs (matrix 0.022, scalar 0.02, embed 0.6, head 0.008) should not need re-tuning. Motivation: (a) linear warmdown has a discontinuous derivative at frac=1-wd (LR drops from 1.0-rate constant to -1/wd slope); cosine is C¹ there. (b) late-QAT flip introduces a loss-landscape shift; cosine's soft LR approach to 0 dampens the optimizer response during QAT settling. (c) Muon momentum is at peak 0.99 in this region — smoother LR reduces buffer-driven overshoot under high momentum. No other knobs touched.

hypothesis: Bump EMA decay 0.9965→0.998 to widen averaging window into more of the 72% warmdown tail.
notes: Current warmdown_frac=0.72 is an unusually long LR cooldown. At ema_decay=0.9965 the EMA half-life is ~198 steps (~4-5% of ~4500-step run), so EMA only averages the last ~1000 steps — a small slice of the stable low-LR regime. Raising to 0.998 (half-life ~346 steps, ~8% of run) captures roughly the last ~1700 steps, still well inside the warmdown region and avoiding the higher-LR pre-warmdown updates. Small-radius meta tweak; expected -0.001 bpb with minimal risk. If this regresses, signal is that faster EMA (e.g. 0.995) is preferred given this warmdown shape.

hypothesis: Per-row MinMSE scale refinement in GPTQ: 11-point α-grid search around the clip_sigmas heuristic (α=1.0 included, so ≥ baseline fidelity); zero byte overhead since scale storage shape is unchanged.
notes: Baseline uses s = clip_sigmas * row_std / clip_range, a fixed heuristic applied uniformly to every row. Real weight rows differ in kurtosis/skew. I scan α ∈ {0.4, 0.55, 0.7, 0.82, 0.9, 0.96, 1.0, 1.04, 1.1, 1.2, 1.35} per row and keep the α that minimises |w − q·s|² (direct weight MSE). Since α=1.0 is in the grid, worst-case is matching baseline. No change to storage layout (still fp16 per-row s) → model_bytes unchanged; code grows by ~20 lines. Runs on CPU in <3s across all matrices. Hessian-aware error correction in the subsequent GPTQ loop is untouched — this only refines the quantization codebook step-size. Standard trick in AdaQuant / OBQ literature; high-confidence small bpb win.

hypothesis: Reset EMA accumulator at warmdown entry so the final averaged weights reflect only the warmdown phase (LR decaying, refining around local min) — avoids dragging in pre-warmdown high-LR exploration noise.

notes: Single-literal change at train_gpt.py:150 (MUON_MOMENTUM default 0.99→0.995). Zero code-byte growth. Rebased workdir to exp_108 first (workdir had stale exp_128 defaults: warmup_fraction=0.15). With warmdown_frac=0.72 and exp_010 linear cool 0.99→0.92, raising peak to 0.995 widens the warmdown cool range (0.995→0.92) by 0.005 but preserves the warmdown floor unchanged. hypothesis: Mask the first half (positions 0..1023) of each TTT training sequence via ignore_index=-100 so TTT gradient comes only from high-context positions, aligning it with the eval distribution where scored tokens always have >=1984 tokens of context.
notes: Single-site edit in eval_val_ttt's inner training loop (~line 1312). y = local[1:].reshape(-1, seq_len).clone() (clone to avoid corrupting reused local); y[:, :seq_len//2] = -100. F.cross_entropy in GPT.forward uses reduction='mean' with default ignore_index=-100, so masked positions contribute zero gradient and are excluded from the denominator. Rationale: TTT currently averages CE over all 2048 positions, but scored eval positions (sliding window stride=64) always have context_size=2048-64=1984 tokens of prior context. Training signal from positions 0..1023 (context <=1023) is a structurally different prediction task from what eval measures. Halving the contributing positions concentrates TTT adaptation on the high-context regime that matches the metric. Compute cost: essentially zero — forward pass unchanged, CE mean now over half the positions (slightly cheaper). Eval-budget-neutral. Novel direction: prior TTT trials tuned LR/momentum/epochs/chunk-size/param-groups; none targeted the train/eval distribution mismatch.

hypothesis: Raise min_lr 0.0→0.05 to floor the linear warmdown LR at 5% of peak in the final ~3.6% of training, preserving late-training refinement into the EMA tail (half-life ~200 steps under decay=0.9965) — completely untouched knob in 140 trials.
notes: Single-literal edit at train_gpt.py:143 (MIN_LR default 0.0→0.05). MIN_LR is NOT a harness-injected env var so changing the Python default takes effect. Zero code-byte growth; zero step-speed impact. With warmdown_frac=0.72 the linear decay lr_mul=(1-frac)/0.72 hits 0.05 at frac=0.964, so only the last ~3.6% of training steps are clipped. EMA_DECAY=0.9965 half-life ~200 steps ≈ 5% of a ~4000-step run, meaning those clipped steps still materially weight the averaged weights. Hypothesis: keeping a small LR alive in that window lets the EMA tail capture continued refinement instead of frozen weights. Risks: (a) floor muddies warmdown convergence and hurts; (b) delta is too small to measure (near-zero). Expected delta −0.0002; acceptable outcome is any net-negative or neutral signal that motivates a follow-up sweep on min_lr ∈ {0.02, 0.05, 0.1}.

hypothesis: Targeted Muon weight-decay hike (muon_wd_recurrent=0.1235, 1.3x muon_wd) on recurrent-segment blocks [loop_start..loop_end] only, since these are traversed (num_loops+1)=3x per forward and receive ~3x more gradient signal per step than non-recurrent blocks — equalizing shrinkage-per-gradient-step is a structurally-motivated split never tried in this tree.
notes: Split matrix_params into recurrent (name starts with 3. through 5.) and other; Muon gets two param groups with weight_decay=0.1235 (recurrent, ~25% of matrix params) vs 0.095 (other). Net effective muon_wd is +7.5%, concentrated on the 3 shared blocks. Distinct from exp_077/095 (+21% global hike, discard), exp_118 (QK-specific hike, discard), exp_082 (adam_wd cut). Motivation is WD-per-gradient-step equalization, not raw WD magnitude. Base_lr propagation covered by existing loop. Compile-safe. Size preflight: code=22,258B, total ~15.80MB.

hypothesis: Latin-square deterministic phase cycling in ShuffledSequenceLoader: per-shard, cycle through 8 evenly-spaced phase buckets in a random per-shard order, replacing the per-shard-epoch uniform-random phase choice — reduces phase-coverage variance when a shard only gets 1-3 epochs within the 600s budget.
notes: Two-site edit to ShuffledSequenceLoader: (1) __init__ adds self._phase_epoch=[0]*N and self._phase_order=[rng.permutation(8).tolist()]*N; (2) _reset_shard replaces `phase = int(rng.integers(max_phase+1))` with deterministic bucket selection `phase = k*(max_phase+1)//8` where k cycles through the per-shard random permutation of 8 buckets. Keeps tile structure (stride=seq_len) intact — unlike exp_131 (fully-random per-seq, discard) which destroyed tiling, and unlike exp_047 (stride//2, discard) which doubled samples with overlap. Motivation: with ~1-2 shards/rank and ~1-3 shard-epochs/shard in 600s, uniform-random phase has high variance — 3 random draws from [0,2048) may cluster. Latin-square guarantees the first K visits land in K distinct buckets, stratifying which token-positions act as "sequence start" across limited epochs. Novel in tree; curr domain has no keeps yet.

hypothesis: Init resid_mix[1] (x0/embedding injection scale) from 0.0→0.05 per-layer: give every block a weak embedding-bypass channel from step 0 so gradient has informative signal immediately; with 3-layer recurrence (layers 3-5 called 3x) every loop pass sees x0 re-injected consistently from the start.
notes: Single-site init change at train_gpt.py:547 (resid_mix construction). Zero params added, zero code-byte growth (~80 bytes of comment + literal swap). Gradient is free to drive back to 0 if harmful. Revives the direction of exp_079 (proposed 0.1, harness_abort → never ran) with conservative 0.05 — distinct datapoint. Baseline exp_108 has resid_mix[1]=0 at init. Key assumption: x is accumulated residual with large norm; x0 is rms_norm'd embedding with norm ~1, so 0.05*x0 is a weak perturbation unlikely to destabilize early training.

hypothesis: Focal-style (γ=1) per-token CE reweighting, mean-normalized: w=(1-p_correct) under no_grad, then normalized so focal_w.mean()=1 to preserve overall gradient magnitude (LR schedule stays calibrated). Redirects gradient budget from saturated easy tokens (common function words, trailing punctuation) toward content tokens where ~36M-param capacity is information-limited.
notes: Structurally distinct from every prior loss trial in the tree. Prior loss trials are all SYMMETRIC token reshapings: exp_009 z-loss (logsumexp penalty across all tokens), exp_042 label smoothing (target-side uniform smear), exp_124 CE clamp(max=7.0) (upper-cap, catastrophic +0.22), exp_132 entropy bonus (output distribution flattening, symmetric). Position-weighting (exp_034) was never executed (harness_abort). Per-token DIFFICULTY reweighting has never been tried. Mechanism: at ~36M params over ~1.5B tokens, easy function-word tokens (articles, punctuation, common affixes) saturate to high p_correct very early in training; their CE gradient contribution then drops to ~0 anyway, but still occupies ~half the mean-normalization denominator. Under focal w=(1-p_correct), these tokens get weight near 0 post-normalization, shifting the effective token budget toward harder content tokens where gradient is still informative. Mean-normalization (divide by focal_w.mean()) is critical: without it, overall gradient magnitude would decay as the model improves (p_correct rises), drifting the LR curriculum. With normalization, gradient SCALE stays fixed and only the PER-TOKEN distribution shifts. no_grad on focal_w ensures no second-order path through p_correct into weights. Risk: with γ=1 and typical p_correct≈0.05 → focal_w≈0.95 for most tokens but ≈0 for the ~30-40% easy tokens, normalization amplifies hard tokens ~1.5-2×, which may increase per-batch loss variance. If variance is an issue, γ=0.5 would soften; γ=2 would sharpen. γ=1 is the standard default and the natural first-test operating point.

hypothesis: Replace GPTQ per-row σ-based SDClip scale (clip_sigmas*row_std/clip_range) with max-abs scale (row.abs().amax()/clip_range): uses full int6/int8 range for every row with zero clipping, ~3× finer quantization bins on non-outlier rows where current clip_sigmas=12.85σ wastes ~70% of int6 range.
notes: Single-site edit at gptq_quantize_weight lines 948-949. clip_sigmas parameter now unused but kept in signature for API compatibility (still passed by gptq_mixed_quantize). Size-neutral: scale tensor is still [rows] float16. Affects both matrices (int6, clip_sigmas=12.85 removed) and tok_emb (int8, clip_sigmas=20.0 removed) uniformly. Risk: rows with extreme outliers will underutilize bins for bulk weights — but SDClip with clip_sigmas=12.85 already effectively never clipped (~99.99% of rows have max < 12.85σ), so max-abs is strictly higher resolution for the common case. Novel: exp_004/008/045 all refined scales *within* the SDClip framework (α-grids, closed-form post-pass); none replaced the σ-based formula with max-abs. Untried knob.

hypothesis: Add a learnable 8192-dim output logit bias applied AFTER the tanh softcap: frees tied tok_emb rows from encoding unigram frequency via their norms (unconstrained log-prior shifts live in the bias), moving that capacity to directional/semantic content while the softcap still bounds the inner-product term.
notes: Novel tree-wide: exp_023 (harness_abort, never executed) proposed a similar learnable bias but gave no signal. Three-site edit: (1) new nn.Parameter(zeros) of shape [8192] on GPT with dtype=float32; (2) forward_logits adds it AFTER softcap so bias adds unconstrained shifts, softcap still caps inner-product; (3) parameter registered in scalar_params (lr=scalar_lr=0.02, wd=adam_wd=0.02, betas). Size: 8192-elem param → numel<=65536 passthrough branch in gptq_mixed_quantize → fp16 (~16KB raw, ~10-12KB after byte-shuffle+brotli11). Code grew ~400 bytes. Fits headroom (prior art shows ~180KB headroom). Rationale: tied-embed outputs are x·E_j + logit_softcap-bounded; currently tok_emb rows must jointly encode direction AND magnitude (frequency). A dedicated bias gives the unigram prior a free home. At ~36M params and ≤1 epoch, this should free ~0.5-2% of tok_emb capacity for semantics. Post-softcap placement is deliberate: pre-softcap, a large bias magnitude (up to ~log(vocab)≈9) would push the tanh deep into saturation, killing gradient to tok_emb.

hypothesis: Cosine-cool TTT SGD momentum 0.9→0 across chunks in lockstep with the existing cos_lr schedule, so late low-LR chunks effectively do vanilla SGD and the momentum buffer can't carry stale early-chunk grads into the precision-adaptation tail.
notes: Revives exp_037 (harness_abort, never ran on GPU). Single-site edit at eval_val_ttt (~line 1296): factor out cos_frac = 0.5*(1+cos(pi*ci/(N-1))) used for both pg["lr"] = ttt_lr*cos_frac and pg["momentum"] = 0.9*cos_frac. First chunk keeps current behavior (cos_frac=1.0 → lr=0.005, mom=0.9). Last chunk: lr=0, mom=0. Motivation: exp_039 raised mom 0.9→0.95 and got +0.000178 (worse), indicating less momentum is directionally correct. In the 4-SGD-steps-per-chunk regime, a persistent 0.9 momentum buffer integrates grads from ~10 chunks back — stale in the late tail where cos_lr is tiny. No change to param count, code bytes, or eval compute (just scalar updates per chunk).

hypothesis: Shrink warmdown_frac 0.72→0.60 to expand the peak-LR+peak-momentum window 6%→18% of training — frac=[0.22,0.40] at peak-LR+peak-momentum instead of only [0.22,0.28], tripling the window exp_088 opened by shrinking Muon momentum warmup 0.33→0.22.
notes: warmdown_frac has never been directly swept in this tree. exp_022 tried 0.72→0.65 but was harness_abort (never ran on GPU). exp_088 WIN (Muon momentum warmup 0.33→0.22) pulled the end-of-momentum-warmup to frac=0.22, meaning the "real peak-momentum window" before warmdown begins is only [0.22, 1-0.72]=[0.22, 0.28] — just 6% of budget. Shortening warmdown to 0.60 extends peak window to [0.22, 0.40] = 18% of budget, 3× exposure. Warmdown tail is still 1200+ steps at ~2000 total steps, plenty for EMA (half-life ~200 steps at decay=0.9965) to converge. Risks: (a) less tail time might hurt EMA quality; (b) coupling with Muon momentum cool-down schedule (exp_010) — the cool now happens over a shorter window so momentum drops faster per-step. Single-literal change at train_gpt.py:108. Zero param/byte growth.

hypothesis: Revive orphaned exp_003 keep on current best lineage: EMA_DECAY 0.9965 to 0.998.

Add V-norm (F.rms_norm on v over head_dim) symmetric with existing Q/K-norm in CausalSelfAttention.forward: bounds attention-output magnitude independent of V-row outliers, completing the Q/K/V normalization triad (Kimi-K2/Qwen3 style).
notes: Single-line insertion at CausalSelfAttention.forward (~line 510): `v = F.rms_norm(v, (v.size(-1),))` placed immediately after existing q/k RMSNorms, BEFORE flash_attn_3_func. Zero parameters added (F.rms_norm is scale-free here — the RMSNorm class has no learnable weight). XSA (_xsa_efficient) is compatible: it uses F.normalize(v, dim=-1) internally which is direction-only, idempotent to an RMS rescaling. Prior arch tree has extensively tested Q-side/residual knobs (rope_dims, resid_mix init, parallel_residual_start, skip_gates), but V-norm specifically — the missing third leg of the QKV-norm triad — has never been proposed. Rationale: under q_gain=5.25 + QK-norm the attention is strongly sharpened; attention output magnitude is therefore dominated by argmax V-row magnitudes rather than averaged, so unnormalized V-outliers can inject residual-stream spikes. V-norm bounds per-head attention output to RMS≈1, delegating magnitude control entirely to the per-channel attn_scale param (init 1.0) which is already learnable. Expected compression impact: negligible — v's activation statistics shift but the c_v projection weight distribution is unchanged during training, and GPTQ calibrates on the new activation path coherently. Rebased workdir to exp_142 first (best snapshot) to drop stale exp_143 resid_mix edit.

hypothesis: Enable Nesterov momentum on the TTT SGD optimizer (nesterov=True) so the ~16 SGD steps/chunk lookahead the next-step gradient, giving effectively near-second-order dynamics in this extremely-low-step regime where classical-momentum staleness dominates.
notes: Single-flag change at eval_val_ttt line 1254 (SGD constructor). Rebased workdir to exp_142 first to wipe stale -100 mask left from exp_139. exp_031 proposed Nesterov previously but was harness_abort and never actually ran, so this is a genuinely untested direction. Orthogonal to exp_039 (which raised momentum scalar 0.9→0.95 and was worse) — Nesterov fixes the update rule, not the scalar. Zero param change, zero training-time change, zero model-bytes change. Risk: with grad-clip=1.0 already applied, Nesterov's lookahead magnitude is bounded so divergence is unlikely.</notes> </invoke>

hypothesis: Exclude Muon matrix params from global grad_clip_norm — Newton-Schulz already bounds per-layer Muon update spectrum, so global L2 clipping couples Muon grads to sparse tok_emb/lm_head outliers and damps them unnecessarily; clip only Adam-managed (tok_emb + lm_head + scalar/control) params at the same 0.3 threshold.
notes: Single-site edit in train_model: precompute _non_muon_params (filter out ids from optimizer_muon.param_groups) once before step_fn, swap the single clip_grad_norm_ call target from base_model.parameters() to _non_muon_params. Threshold unchanged (h.grad_clip_norm=0.3). Zero param-count change, ~400 bytes of code growth. Distinct from all prior opt trials (which all touched schedule shape, momentum peak/warmup, or weight-decay targeting — never the clip target set). Risk: if clip was silently protecting against embedding-driven instability by damping the WHOLE net's early grads, isolating clip to the Adam side could let Muon take a single bad step early. Mitigation: Newton-Schulz outputs are bounded in norm regardless of input grad magnitude, so Muon's actual p.add_ magnitude per step is bounded by lr * aspect_ratio^0.5 ≈ matrix_lr * 1.0 = 0.022, far below the clip-equivalent scale.

hypothesis: Tighten matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→10.5 — direct SDClip scale tightening (18% smaller quant scale → finer int6 resolution for bulk Gaussian weights) without the aggressive per-row MinMSE that size-blocked exp_145/exp_004/exp_008.
notes: Baseline SDClip int6 uses matrix_clip_sigmas=12.85 (very conservative: sf=12.85*std/31=0.414*std, typical |w|=0.8σ → int≈1.9). P(|w|>12.85σ) ≈ 10^-38 for Gaussian so NO clipping happens — 12.85 is purely a grid-coarseness choice. Muon-trained matrix weights are Gaussian-like with row_max/row_std≈3.5, so clip_sigmas well above ~6 has zero clipping probability. Dropping to 10.5 (18% tighter scale: sf=0.339σ, |w|=0.8σ→int≈2.4) still has P(clip)<10^-25 for Gaussian, but gives strictly finer resolution for bulk weights → better post-quant MSE → lower bpb. Size cost comes only from slightly higher Q-value entropy (spread from mostly-{0,±1} toward {0,±1,±2,±3}), expected 20-60 KB within the ~180 KB headroom. Differs from prior quant attempts (exp_145 max-abs, exp_008 per-layer α-grid, exp_004 per-row MinMSE) which all went MUCH more aggressive (effective clip_sigmas≈3.5) and size-blocked. This is a calibrated middle ground — the direct constant sweep has never been tried at any specific intermediate value.

hypothesis: Add uniform intra-bucket jitter to exp_142's Latin-square phase cycling: each reset samples jitter∈[0,bucket_width) on top of the bucket offset so repeat visits to the same bucket k land on a DIFFERENT actual phase. Preserves Latin-square coverage guarantee at bucket granularity while extending phase coverage from 8 discrete points to continuous over [0,max_phase).
notes: Single-site edit in ShuffledSequenceLoader._reset_shard (~line 367). Without jitter, the 9th+ epoch of a shard revisits bucket k with IDENTICAL start_inds set (only permutation order differs) — so model sees exactly the same tokens in shuffled order. Jitter via self.rng.integers(0, bucket_width) makes every repeat visit sample a fresh phase within the bucket, giving genuinely-new stream cuts. bucket_width=(seq_len)//8=256 so jitter range is [0,256). Zero params, ~600 bytes of code growth (mostly comment).

hypothesis: Continuous input-embedding Gaussian noise σ=0.015 added to post-RMSNorm x in forward_logits, train-only via self.training gate — a smoother alternative to exp_040's failed MLP hidden dropout and exp_012's size-blocked discrete token-replacement.
notes: One-line addition at train_gpt.py:~672 inside GPT.forward_logits: `if self.training: x = x + torch.randn_like(x) * 0.015`. Post-RMSNorm x has unit RMS so σ=0.015 = ~1.5% perturbation per element. Zero new params; ~120 code bytes (comment + line). Gated on self.training so: (1) val/eval passes see zero noise — no metric contamination, (2) TTT-score pass under inference_mode sees zero noise — legal, (3) TTT-train inner step sees noise — acts as mild chunk-overfitting regularizer. Structural motivation: prior reg trials show dropout-style disruption HURTS at this param count (exp_040: +0.0098) while targeted weight-regularization HELPS (exp_141: -0.00027). Continuous Gaussian noise on embeddings is between these — preserves gradient topology (unlike dropout masks) but still smooths the embedding geometry. Risk: model may need extra redundancy to denoise, slightly inflating brotli-compressed model bytes (exp_012 was size_blocked for a similar reason). If size_blocks, next iter tries σ=0.008.

hypothesis: Gentle position-weighted CE (linspace 0.9→1.1 across T=2048, mean-preserving): aligns training gradient with eval's stride-64 scored regime [1984,2047] by a 20% amplitude tilt toward late positions, structurally distinct from prior per-token (exp_144 focal), target-side (exp_042 smoothing), and symmetric (exp_009 z-loss) loss trials.
notes: Single-site edit in CasualDecoder.forward (train_gpt.py lines 705-709). Replaces plain mean-reduction F.cross_entropy with a per-token CE (reduction="none") multiplied by a position weight vector w(t)=linspace(0.9,1.1,T) broadcast across batch, then mean-reduced. Key property: mean(linspace(0.9,1.1,T))==1.0 exactly, so the global gradient magnitude is preserved — the existing LR schedule (warmdown fraction, Muon momentum ramp, etc.) stays calibrated. Zero new params, zero compile-graph hazards (pw is a fresh tensor each forward, no mutable state). Compute cost negligible: one extra linspace+expand (no additional matmul). Motivation: eval scores only positions [1984,2047] (stride-64 sliding window, context_size=1984); training CE averages over all 2048 positions, giving the eval regime only 3.1% of the gradient. 20% amplitude slope shifts roughly 10% of gradient mass toward the eval-target tail. Contrast with exp_034 (linspace 0.5→1.5, 100% amplitude, harness_abort): 5× gentler to stay in small-perturbation regime and avoid training instability. Structural novelty vs all executed loss trials: exp_009 z-loss (symmetric logsumexp² regularizer), exp_042 label_smoothing down (target-side symmetric), exp_144 focal γ=1 (per-token difficulty, position-agnostic). This is the first POSITION-based loss reshape actually to execute.

hypothesis: Temperature sharpening T=0.97 on scoring logits in eval_val_ttt (divide logits by 0.97 before CE) — opposite direction of exp_044 (T=1.05 worsened +0.00261), motivated by GPTQ int6 weight noise flattening the output distribution.

Applied ONLY to the scoring cross_entropy (drives reported val_bpb), NOT to the TTT training CE at line 1322 so the gradient signal is unchanged. Rationale: exp_044 established bpb is highly sensitive to T (Δ=+0.00261 for T=1.05). By local quadratic assumption, if optimum T*<1 (as GPTQ softening suggests), T=0.97 should improve over T=1.0 — if T*≈1.0 this costs ~0.0009, so 50/50 risk with asymmetric upside. Size/time impact: zero (single fp32 division).

hypothesis: Zipf-weighted row-magnitude init for tied tok_emb: after normal_ init, multiply row i by 1/sqrt(1 + i/1024) so rare high-ID tokens start with smaller norms. Tied embeddings feed F.linear for output logits (row-norm-sensitive), so rare-token logits start small → low-confidence uniform prior for rares at t=0, matching BPB's objective.
notes: Single-site edit in _init_weights (GPT class). Distinct from prior tok experiments: exp_011 (raised std uniformly, size_blocked), exp_019 (equi-row-norm sphere, discard), exp_038 (lowered std uniformly, discard), exp_032 (trunc_normal same std, never ran), exp_146 (learnable output bias, eval_budget_overrun). None tried rank-graded magnitude. Zero added params. Shrinks norms (never grows), so GPTQ quant range narrows → no size risk. SP8192 BPE roughly frequency-orders IDs, so low-rank rows stay at full init for common tokens while high-rank rows ≈1/3× init_std ≈ lm_head-style near-zero for rarest.

hypothesis: Bump Adam beta2 0.95→0.98: tied embedding (4.2M params) sees sparse per-row gradients; longer 2nd-moment half-life (~35 vs ~14 steps) stabilizes Adam's preconditioner for long-tail vocab rows.

Affects optimizer_tok (tied embedding, 4.2M params, sparse gradients) and optimizer_scalar (tiny dense-gradient groups — near-no-op). optimizer_head is None under tie_embeddings=True. muon_beta2 is declared but UNUSED (verified: no h.muon_beta2 consumer), so this change is Adam-only. Untried direction on tree: exp_021 (harness_abort, never ran GPU) proposed cosine-cooling beta2 downward. Size risk low — smoother preconditioning should produce steadier embedding weights, not larger ones, unlike exp_149 (EMA_DECAY bump, size_blocked) which averaged more of the early-training trajectory.

hypothesis: Restore orphaned exp_005 win: rope_dims 16→32 (50% partial RoPE). exp_005 proved -0.000788 on the older stack but the change was lost at exp_014 when a sibling specialist rebased from a pre-exp_005 ancestor; every subsequent keep (including current best exp_142) inherits rope_dims=16.

No later keep has touched rope_dims, so the change is safely orthogonal to exp_088 (Muon momentum fraction), exp_108 (GPTQ block), exp_141 (Muon WD recurrent), exp_142 (loader phase cycling). Risk: diminished magnitude on a tighter baseline (1.0766 vs 1.0810 at exp_005 time).

hypothesis: Add weight_decay=1e-4 to the TTT SGD(Nesterov) optimizer so the L2 pull dampens Nesterov-amplified cumulative drift across ~176 SGD steps (11 chunks × 16 steps), preventing late-chunk divergence without an explicit weight anchor.

Rationale: current best (exp_151) enabled Nesterov, which amplifies update magnitudes via look-ahead momentum. Over 11 TTT chunks the Nesterov-accelerated updates can compound parameter drift in tok_emb / attention matrices; small WD=1e-4 provides a gentle L2 pull that counters this without the semantics of an anchor toward pretrained weights (pulls toward 0 instead). Distinct from prior TTT trials: exp_039 (momentum↑, discarded), exp_049 (freeze tok_emb, discarded), exp_147 (momentum decay, discarded), exp_151 (Nesterov, kept). No prior trial tested WD in the TTT inner loop. Risk: tok_emb shrinkage could hurt softmax sharpness; 1e-4 × lr 0.005 × 16 steps ≈ 8e-6 cumulative relative shrink per chunk, negligible.

hypothesis: Replace exp_142's independent per-shard random 8-bucket permutations with a deterministic bit-reversal order [0,4,2,6,1,5,3,7] offset by shard index (si%8) — low-discrepancy guarantees within-shard (first k visits maximally span phase space) and guaranteed cross-shard coverage at any epoch index.
notes: Single-site edit at ShuffledSequenceLoader.__init__ (~line 361). exp_142 (curr, keep -0.000028) introduced per-shard random permutations of 8 phase buckets; exp_154 (curr, discard +0.000683) added within-bucket jitter and hurt. This trial keeps the clean exp_142 phase-grid structure but replaces random per-shard permutations with a bit-reversal permutation (canonical 1D low-discrepancy on powers of 2) cycled with a per-shard offset. Two guarantees vs exp_142: (a) within-shard, for ANY k<=8 visits to a shard, the k buckets used are the first k of the bit-reversal order — so the two extreme phases {0, max/2} are ALWAYS sampled in the first two epochs instead of probabilistically missed under random permutation, and the quarter-points {max/4, 3*max/4} are ALWAYS sampled by epoch 4. Under 600s budget ~2-3 epochs/shard, this fixes exp_142's coupon-collector variance. (b) cross-shard: at any epoch index j, the phases used by 8 consecutive shards form a full bit-reversal permutation of {0..7}, so any window of 8 shards covers all buckets exactly once. Baseline (rng.permutation) at epoch 0 across 12 shards covers all 8 buckets with probability ~0.80 (Stirling). Zero params, ~400B code growth (mostly comment). Model bytes identical (no model change). RNG stream still consumed for sequence_order shuffle inside _reset_shard and shard draw in next_batch — only the _phase_order allocation differs.

hypothesis: Cosine-symmetric linear cool of AdamW beta1 0.9→0.8 during LR warmdown (lockstep with lr_scale), on tok_emb + scalar AdamW groups. Unlike exp_075 (static beta1=0.8, discarded +0.00127) this preserves beta1=0.9 during peak-LR and only contracts in warmdown; unlike exp_021 (beta2 cool, harness_abort) it targets the first moment which dominates update direction.

Applies to tok_emb AdamW, scalar AdamW, and lm_head Adam (latter is None when tie_embeddings=True). During the 20-step warmup phase (lr_scale=1.0, elapsed_ms=0), beta1_cooled=0.9 → no behavior change. During 28% peak-LR, lr_scale=1.0 → beta1=0.9 identical to baseline. Only in the 72% linear warmdown does beta1 ramp 0.9→0.8. Symmetric with exp_010's muon_momentum cool (0.99→warmup_start=0.92) also linear in lr_scale. Adam's first moment is stored in optimizer state (unaffected by EMA), so this alters update DIRECTION not magnitude at tail.

hypothesis: Split Muon weight decay by parameter TYPE: keep muon_wd=0.095 on attention matrices, raise to 0.125 (1.32x) on MLP matrices only — MLPs are 2.7× the attention-matrix mass and the dominant overfit risk.

Implementation: two new `muon_wd_mlp` hparam + split in Optimizers.__init__ + list-of-dicts to Muon constructor; standard PyTorch param-group semantics (defaults merge into groups, overrides win). Risks: if Muon's step() has subtle multi-group behavior I missed, crash. If MLP WD=0.125 is too aggressive globally (vs. exp_141's 3-layer subset), mild regression instead of improvement.

hypothesis: Pair brotli lgwin=22→24 (max 16 MB compression window vs default 4 MB) with modest matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→12.0 tightening — the larger window captures cross-matrix redundancy in the ~27 MB quant dict to fund finer GPTQ int6 resolution without exceeding the 16 MB cap.

If size still blocks, the lgwin=24 savings + residual overflow give the team a precise budget number for the next specialist to plan around. If keep, confirms both axes simultaneously.

hypothesis: Add a learnable per-vocab output bias (8192 fp32 scalars) applied AFTER logit softcap: logits = softcap*tanh(F.linear(x, tok_emb.weight)/softcap) + out_bias, zero-init, in scalar_params optimizer group. Decouples the log-unigram/marginal-frequency prior from tied tok_emb rows so embedding geometry specializes on token direction rather than absorbing both direction + frequency scale.

hypothesis: Eval-stride-aligned sigmoid position weight: mean-preserving S-curve centered at t=T-64 with slope 1/16 and amplitude 0.3, concentrating the gradient tilt at the exact eval-stride boundary (bulk ≈0.99, tail peaks ≈1.29) instead of exp_156's uniform linspace(0.9,1.1) spread.

Mean-preserving by construction (antisymmetric offset). Motivation: val_bpb sliding-window stride=64 means scored positions always have context-depth ≥ 1984; concentrating the tilt at that exact boundary targets eval alignment more precisely than spreading ±10% across all 2048 positions. Amplitude 0.3 on ~3% of positions keeps total gradient perturbation moderate — tail boost ~1.3x vs exp_156's 1.1x. Structurally distinct shape change (S-curve vs linear ramp), not a linspace-amplitude sweep. Smooth sigmoid avoids exp_066's hard-mask failure mode at stride boundary.

hypothesis: Pure sliding-window semantics: first window (ws=0) now scores only positions [context_size:wlen] like all subsequent windows, eliminating low-context warmup tokens (positions 0..1983) from val_bpb so every scored token has uniform >=1984-token context (HF-style perplexity convention).

Legal: sliding-window at stride=64 is preserved; this is the STANDARD HF-style perplexity convention (each scored token has >=context_size prior tokens). hypothesis: Tighten grad_clip_norm 0.3→0.2 (−33%): the 3-layer recurrent segment 3×-amplifies gradient norms in loop layers so the current 0.3 clip attenuates them proportionally less than non-recurrent layers — tighter clipping complements exp_141's recurrent-WD hike on the gradient side.

hypothesis: Fatter heads: num_heads 8→4, num_kv_heads 4→2 (head_dim 64→128, param-neutral), rope_dims 32→64 to preserve exp_005's 50% partial-RoPE coverage under the new head size.

hypothesis: Reset SGD momentum buffers at each TTT chunk boundary (clear optimizer.state['momentum_buffer'] after setting cos_lr): each chunk is a distinct text region, and with only ~8-16 SGD steps/chunk at momentum=0.9 the tail velocity from the previous chunk biases the early updates of the new chunk.

Mechanism: the cosine LR schedule (peak at ci=0, 0 at ci=num-1) already implies each chunk is "fresh", but the momentum buffer silently carries velocity forward, so at ci=2 with cos_lr at ~75% of peak the first SGD step still has effective velocity from ci=1's ~100% peak LR direction. Distinct from exp_163 (beta1 cool, discarded delta=+0.000146) since wd acts on weight magnitudes, not gradient moments, and targets quantization error directly. Active only during warmdown (lr_scale<1.0); peak phase unchanged. -0.0004 discard 1.076401 +0.000334 15993633 600.098000 769.902000 1484.000000 apg-opt-0167

Further split Muon attention WD: Q/K projections get 0.125 (match MLP), V + attn.proj stay at muon_wd=0.095. Q/K have RMS-norm post-projection so their weight norms don't drive score magnitude — aggressive WD is cheap directional regularization.

MLP unchanged at 0.125. Structurally orthogonal to exp_164 (mlp-vs-attn split, kept) and exp_141 (depth split, superseded). Rationale: lines 514-515 apply F.rms_norm(q/k, (head_dim,)) after the c_q/c_k projections, so weight norms of c_q/c_k are invariant up to direction at forward time — WD on them acts primarily as a soft directional prior rather than a magnitude squeeze. V and proj lack this renormalization so their WD tradeoff is more delicate; leave them alone. Expected: small further gain (exp_164 itself was -0.000024, this is a finer sub-split of the same bucket).

hypothesis: Round-robin stratified shard sampling in next_batch (with per-batch random rotation offset) replaces weighted-random independent per-slot draws, eliminating per-batch shard-composition multinomial variance (~50% CoV with 12 shards × 48 slots).
notes: Structural change to ShuffledSequenceLoader.next_batch only. Per-shard _reset_shard phase bucketing (Latin-square over 8 buckets) is unchanged, so within-shard temporal phase coverage is preserved. Within a shard, sequence ordering is still randomized by rng.permutation. Distinct axis from all three prior curr keeps/discards (exp_142 Latin-square keep, exp_154 jitter discard, exp_162 bit-reversal discard — all intra-shard temporal tweaks). With device_batch_size ≈ 48 and ~12 shards/rank: previously each shard contributed ~4 sequences/batch with stddev ≈ 2 (multinomial over 48 draws); round-robin pins this at exactly 4 per shard per batch (empty shards skipped). Gradient estimation variance due to shard-ID bias per step should drop, sharpening the effective per-step update direction. Trade-off: removes proportional-to-remaining bias, so near-end-of-epoch small shards are visited at the same rate as large shards until they empty — on balance expected to help because shards are near-equal-size in FineWeb10B. RNG call frequency drops from device_batch_size → 1 per batch (fewer entropy consumers, identical determinism guarantees).

hypothesis: Tighten GPTQ matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→12.5 (halfway toward exp_165's 12.0): finer int6 resolution on ~29M matrix params, isolated without exp_165's lgwin=24 change that pushed bytes over the 16MB cap.

hypothesis: Stack exp_156's gentle position-weighted CE (linspace 0.9→1.1, mean-preserving) onto exp_164 (current best, reg-branch split-Muon-WD) — two orthogonal wins (loss shape vs per-group optimizer WD) should combine additively since exp_164 was rooted on a different branch that did not include the position-weight.

Mechanism: val_bpb uses stride-64 sliding window so only the last 64 positions of each window are scored; linspace(0.9,1.1) tilts gradient mass 10% toward the high-context tail where eval actually measures, while mean(pw)=1.0 preserves overall gradient scale and LR calibration. Affects ONLY optimizer_scalar param group — scalar_params are ndim<2 tensors plus skip_weights/skip_gates. qk_gain_init=5.25 and ln_scale_init=1.0 are both far from the WD-preferred 0, so the 0.02 default was likely inherited from a non-learned-scalar context. 4× reduction softens without eliminating (some regularization retained). Zero added params, zero code-byte delta. Expected -0.0003 to -0.0008 (near TTT noise floor but mechanism-consistent).

XSA subtracts each head's V-direction from its output (an anti-self regularizer); on encoder layers this may damage feature construction while on decoder layers it productively forces attention onto other positions. exp_028 tried xsa_last_n=5 but was harness_aborted (never tested); 6 is the clean encoder/decoder cut aligned with num_encoder_layers=11//2=5.

Alternatives considered and rejected: (a) xsa_last_n=7 would straddle encoder/decoder; (b) rope_dims 16→32 already re-tested twice as discard (exp_121, exp_160); (c) SwiGLU MLP would blow size; (d) num_loops 2→3 hit eval_budget_overrun (exp_046); (e) Sub-LN crashed (exp_090); (f) V-norm just discarded (exp_150). This proposal targets a confirmed-abortive direction (exp_028) with a cleaner structural cut.

hypothesis: Soft row-L2 cap at tied tok_emb init: after nn.init.normal_, shrink only the outlier ~5% of rows whose L2 norm exceeds 1.15×expected_norm (=1.15·std·sqrt(d)) down to exactly that cap; the other ~95% of rows keep their natural Gaussian row-norm variation — preserves the log-unigram prior exp_019's strict sphere init destroyed, while killing the long tail that drives per-row int8 quant scales and early-training gradient noise.
notes: Single-site edit in GPT._init_weights (right after the existing nn.init.normal_ on tied tok_emb). Zero added parameters. Init-only compute overhead (one per-row L2 norm + clamp + scale on 8192×512). Novel middle-path vs prior tok-init tries: exp_011 (std 0.005→0.02 uniform = size_blocked), exp_019 (strict sphere / equi-row-norm = discard +0.001603), exp_038 (std→0.003 uniform = discard +0.000526), exp_158 (Zipf-weighted scaling = discard +0.001793). None of these touched ONLY the tail of the row-norm distribution; this one does. Rationale: the 5% outlier rows (row-norm > 1.15·expected) drive embed_clip_sigmas·row_std/clip_range for int8 embed quant and contribute disproportionate early gradient variance; clipping them at init gives cleaner QAT and a tighter post-training row-norm distribution that brotli-11 can compress better. Preserves 95% of rows untouched so the bulk's log-unigram prior (encoded in row-norm differences) survives. If this keeps, natural follow-up is to make the cap ratio (1.15) a tunable knob or anneal it mid-training.

hypothesis: Tighten TTT per-step grad clip 1.0→0.5: pairs with exp_151's Nesterov (kept) by capping outlier lookahead-amplified updates on anomalous val chunks while keeping clip 1.67x training's 0.3.

Motivation: with Nesterov+momentum 0.9, lookahead gradient direction can amplify outlier step magnitudes on unusual chunks (code/non-English in FineWeb val). Zero parameters, zero size impact, zero train-loop impact. Compute delta on eval is sub-millisecond. Orthogonal to exp_014/017 (epochs=4) and exp_151 (Nesterov), composable with current TTT stack.

hypothesis: Halve eval_stride 64→32: doubles scoring-window density so every scored token gets avg 2031.5 context tokens (up from 2015.5, +0.79%), applied uniformly to both quantized_sliding_window and quantized_ttt scoring passes.

Stride=32 doubles scoring forward-pass count (TTT training work unchanged), estimated +60-80s eval wall. context_size auto-follows via `seq_len - stride` = 2016, so scored-position offsets shift to [2016,2048) giving every scored token ≥2016 tokens of context (vs 1984 currently). Each val token still scored exactly once (no multi-window averaging). Budget risk acknowledged — if it tips into eval_budget_overrun, that itself resolves the remaining "what if we doubled scoring density" question in this tree.

hypothesis: Raise AdamW beta2 0.95→0.98 for the tok_emb optimizer only (largest Adam-managed tensor, 4.2M params, Zipfian heavy-tailed grads under tied embeddings) — more stable second-moment denominator on rare-token directions; scalar/head groups keep 0.95.

Motivation: under tie_embeddings the tok_emb matrix is the biggest Adam tensor and its gradient is heavy-tailed (token-frequency Zipfian). beta2=0.95 half-life ≈14 steps under-smooths v; 0.98 (half-life ≈35) gives more stable 1/sqrt(v) preconditioning for rare-token rows while common-token rows still drive magnitude. Orthogonal to all prior opt edits: exp_088 (momentum_warmup_frac), exp_010 (momentum cool), exp_002/043 (cosine WD), exp_163 (beta1 cool discard), exp_172 (muon_wd ramp discard). tok_emb's Adam betas have not been differentiated from scalar group in any prior trial. Zero parameter-count change, zero size impact — pure hyper tweak. Risk: on short (~200-step) runs beta2=0.98 may be too slow to adapt initially; justification is that tok_emb gets dense gradient every step (not sparse under tie_embeddings) so early adaptation isn't the bottleneck, variance is.

hypothesis: Per-module GPTQ clip_sigmas: tighten attn matrices (c_q/c_k/c_v/attn.proj) to 12.0 while keeping MLP/other at 12.85. Captures the bpb win of exp_165 (size_blocked at global 12.0) while limiting the entropy hit to ~27% of matrix params so size stays under 16MB.

The bpb sensitivity to clip_sigmas is proven; the size cost scales with per-matrix entropy times matrix bytes. Attn matrices (512×512, 512×256 x4 per block) are ~27% of matrix params vs MLP's ~73%. Localizing the tighter clip to attention should: (1) cost <27% of exp_165's size overshoot, (2) retain per-param bpb gain since attn errors propagate through the residual stream more broadly than MLP errors. Single-site edit in gptq_mixed_quantize switching on ".attn." in name. Expected Δ ≈ -0.0004: a fraction of exp_165's unobserved delta (would have been ~-0.0008 vs prior best).

hypothesis: Per-matrix Hessian-weighted scale refinement after GPTQ: closed-form solve for scalar α minimizing tr((W−αQ·diag(s)) H (W−αQ·diag(s))^T) and fold into per-row fp16 s (size-neutral).

Given fixed integer codes Q (GPTQ output), α = ((W_perm*(Wq@H)).sum())/((Wq*(Wq@H)).sum()) is the closed-form Hessian-weighted MSE optimum, clamped to [0.75,1.25] as a safety margin. Updates s ← (sf·α).to(fp16) and returns as before. Byte-neutral: Q tensor untouched, s keeps shape/dtype/row-ratios → brotli entropy unchanged. Distinct from prior size_blocked attempts: exp_004 (per-row α-grid), exp_008 (per-layer α-grid stored separately), exp_045 (per-row closed-form). Per-MATRIX (one scalar per weight, ~30 total) absorbed into existing s = no new stored tensors, no entropy change. Rationale: SDClip (clip_sigmas=12.85) is optimal PRE-quantization; GPTQ's error propagation shifts the effective optimum, so an α slightly off 1.0 recovers a small chunk of Hessian-weighted MSE. Compute cost tiny (~30 extra matmuls of size rows×cols×cols at pack time).

Implementation uses the logsumexp form nll=lse-logits.gather(target); uniform_term=lse-logits.mean(-1); loss=(1-eps)*nll+eps*uniform_term. hypothesis: Cross-shard anti-correlated phase cycles: replace per-shard independent permutation(8) with a shared base permutation shifted by shard-index mod 8, so concurrent shards always use distinct phases (eliminating the ~12.5% phase-collision probability per pair) and batches spanning multiple shards see maximally diverse positional offsets.
notes: Single-line edit at ShuffledSequenceLoader.__init__. Before: each shard gets its own rng.permutation(8); at any _phase_epoch value j, two random shards collide on the same phase ~12.5% of the time, reducing within-batch positional-offset diversity. After: one shared _base = rng.permutation(8), and _phase_order[si][j] = (_base[j] + si) % 8. Each shard's 8-reset cycle still visits all 8 phases (permutation property preserved). At any j, shards with distinct (si%8) are GUARANTEED distinct phases. For ~13 shards/rank, first 8 shards collision-free; remaining 5 minimally overlap with first 5 — strictly better cross-shard phase diversity than random. Orthogonal to exp_196 (stratified shard-count quotas) and exp_142 (per-shard deterministic phase cycle — this one is CROSS-shard). Zero cost: no new params, no new data access, no new ops. RNG state advances by 1 permutation call vs len(files) calls — tiny seed-drift but negligible. Expected gain in line with prior curr kept wins (exp_142 -0.000024, exp_196 -0.000087).

hypothesis: Lookahead slow-weights pullback at each TTT chunk boundary: initialize slow_params to pre-TTT snapshot, then after every chunk's SGD steps do slow ← slow + 0.5·(live − slow) followed by live ← slow. Standard Lookahead (Zhang et al. 2019) uses k=5-10 fast steps between pullbacks; our k≈16 steps/chunk matches that scale. α=0.5 is the paper's default; if this keeps, α is a clean follow-up sweep axis. Memory cost: one float32 copy of ttt_params at eval time (≈140 MB, fits). Compute cost: one lerp+copy per chunk (≈13×35M ops = negligible).

hypothesis: Position-weighted Hessian accumulation in GPTQ calibration: scale each token's activation by sqrt(linspace(0.9,1.1,T)) before x.T@x so the Hessian emphasises late-context positions that dominate the stride-64 sliding-window eval — mean-preserving (trace unchanged), size-neutral (calibration only), mirrors exp_176's proven 0.9→1.1 training-CE weighting.

Rationale: eval scores every token under ≥1984 tokens of context (stride-64 sliding window), but current calibration weights all positions equally (t=0 activations have 0 context and don't match eval regime). Gentle 0.9→1.1 ramp matches exp_176/186's winning training-CE schedule — too aggressive (e.g. 0.5→1.5) would distort H's conditioning. hypothesis: Add output-side per-feature bias output_bias_feat (shape=[embedding_dim]=512, init=0) applied to x via F.linear(x + output_bias_feat, tok_emb.weight) before the tied softmax — structural DUAL of input-side embed_scale and mathematically a rank-1 per-vocab logit bias (W @ b) that captures frequency-prior capacity at 512 params instead of the full 8192-dim vocab_bias that size-blocked exp_023/146/166/219/249/277/317.

(1) GPT.__init__: after embed_scale, add self.output_bias_feat = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(h.embedding_dim, dtype=torch.float32)) [512 fp32 params, ~2KB raw, scalar passthrough → ~1KB compressed, well under 180KB headroom]. (2) forward_logits: replace F.linear(x, tok_emb.weight) with F.linear(x + output_bias_feat.to(x.dtype), tok_emb.weight) on the tied branch only — mathematically equivalent to adding (tok_emb.weight @ output_bias_feat) as a per-vocab logit bias, giving a rank-1 frequency-prior term without an 8192-param addition. (3) Optimizers.__init__: append output_bias_feat to scalar_params so it trains in the scalar AdamW group (lr=scalar_lr, wd=adam_wd=0.005) alongside embed_scale/ln_scale/qk_gain. Why 512 and not 8192: the last seven full-vocab-bias proposals (exp_146..exp_317) all tripped size_blocked despite some showing val_bpb in the 1.074-1.076 range. 512 scalars is ~1/16 the byte budget and sits far from the cap. Zero-init makes it a no-op at step 0. Distinct from exp_260 (per-feature output GAIN, discarded): that scaled x multiplicatively; this shifts x additively, and its rank-1-per-vocab equivalence explicitly targets unigram-prior direction in embedding space. TTT-safe: ttt_params = base_model.parameters() already sweeps all nn.Parameters, so it participates in chunk-wise SGD updates. GPTQ-safe: it's a 1-D scalar, not a 2-D matrix, so GPTQ SDClip only touches 2-D Linear/Embedding weights — passthrough fp16.

RMSNorm strips magnitude so skip_weights (direction) and skip_gates (lerp fraction) become orthogonal. Near-equivalent to baseline at init (skip_weights=1, g=0.5), avoiding cold-start destabilization.

Rationale: every Muon momentum experiment that moves toward "more effective averaging" has won (exp_088 faster ramp, exp_010/324 preserve during warmdown); the peak cap at 0.99 is the only direction never probed. If this wins, follow-up can push to 0.995. If it loses, we learn 0.99 is near-optimum.

hypothesis: Latin-square phase count 8→5 (prime, coprime with seq_len=2^11): 3-reset saturation 37.5%→60%, phase spacing 256→410 tokens (adjacent-phase content overlap 88%→80%).

hypothesis: Ledoit-Wolf-style GPTQ Hessian off-diagonal shrinkage (×0.95, diag preserved) after exp_280's diag-only ×1.02 damping — targets finite-sample noise in H's off-diagonal entries that propagates through Hinv into cross-column error compensation.

The 64 calib batches × 2048 tokens ≈ 130K samples estimating a 512² Hessian (~260K unique entries) is moderately under-determined; off-diagonals are the noisiest and directly drive spurious compensation in `W_block[:, j:] -= err * Hinv_block[j, j:]`. Diagonal is untouched so both the existing perm = argsort(H.diag()) ordering and scale calc (row_std/clip_range) are unaffected. Novel axis vs all prior quant trials which only modify H.diagonal() (exp_016/212/280) or GPTQ mechanics (block_size exp_108/261/332, calibration exp_339). Expect near-neutral Q entropy (size), small bpb improvement from stabler Hinv.

hypothesis: Semi-orthogonal init for tied tok_emb.weight (shape [8192, 512]) with gain = tied_embed_init_std * sqrt(vocab_size) ≈ 0.4525 to Frobenius-match prior normal_(std=0.005): the 512 feature columns become orthonormal 8192-dim vocab vectors at t=0, decorrelating the softmax's feature axes before training starts.
notes: Single-site edit in GPT._init_weights at line 723: replace nn.init.normal_(tok_emb.weight, mean=0.0, std=0.005) with nn.init.orthogonal_(tok_emb.weight, gain=0.005*sqrt(8192)). For [M=8192, N=512] with M>=N, orthogonal_ sets W^T W = gain^2 * I_N, making the N=512 feature columns orthonormal 8192-dim vocab vectors. Gain 0.4525 chosen to keep per-entry variance ≈ g^2/M = 2.5e-5 = (0.005)^2, preserving the block[0] residual-RMS statistics that embed_scale + post-tok_emb RMSNorm are tuned for. Structurally novel vs prior tok experiments: exp_019 (discarded) constrained ROWS to uniform L2 norm; exp_011/038 swept init_std magnitude; exp_032/331 clipped Gaussian tails with trunc_normal_. None decorrelated feature-axis columns. Motivation: the tied softmax output logit_i = <x, tok_emb[i,:]> reads features linearly in the 8192×512 matrix; orthonormal columns mean no two features encode redundant vocabulary directions at t=0, removing decorrelation work that Gaussian init defers to training. Risk: interaction with embed_scale (exp_298 gauge-fix inside rms_norm) or with GPTQ int6 codebook — but init affects only t=0 gradient path, not post-training quantized weights. Zero params, zero size delta.

hypothesis: Halve TTT SGD grad_clip_norm 1.0 → 0.5 (single literal at line 1418) — tighter clip should stabilize the first-chunk TTT updates (raw un-adapted model produces noisy gradients that interact with Nesterov@0.9 momentum and 4 epochs of 32-seq batches) while leaving later-chunk updates effectively unchanged (their grad norms are already well below 0.5 after initial adaptation). Zero code-size delta (one character), zero parameter delta, zero train-path impact — only affects TTT SGD updates during quantized_ttt eval. Risk profile: worst case the tighter clip throttles useful mid-chunk updates and bpb drifts up a tick; best case it removes first-chunk overshoot and stabilizes scoring. Also naturally orthogonal to exp_264's alternate-direction traversal (264) — those decorrelate batch ordering, this decorrelates update magnitude.

hypothesis: Rotated-phase depth recurrence: left-rotate loop_seg by iter_idx (iter 0 [3,4,5], iter 1 [4,5,3], iter 2 [5,3,4]), so each loop block visits each loop-position exactly once across the 3 iterations — breaks translational symmetry of identically-repeated loop calls, zero params, zero bytes, preserves exp_245's 1/sqrt(3) correction exactly.
notes: Single-site edit in GPT.__init__ at ~L697: replaced `for _ in range(h.num_loops+1): all_indices.extend(loop_seg)` with left-rotated extension `for iter_idx: shift=iter_idx%seg_len; all_indices.extend(loop_seg[shift:]+loop_seg[:shift])`. Unrolled encoder+decoder: was [0,1,2,3,4,5,3,4,5,3,4,5,6..10], now [0,1,2,3,4,5,4,5,3,5,3,4,6..10]; same length 17, same per-block call count (each loop block 3×). Novel axis vs arch tree: exp_245 (input ln_scale damp, keep), exp_306 (output init damp, discard), exp_321 (learnable per-iter scalar, crash) all modified magnitudes; this is the first edit altering the ORDER of loop calls. No back-to-back same-block at iteration boundaries (last-of-iter0=5, first-of-iter1=4; last-of-iter1=3, first-of-iter2=5). Mechanism: baseline recurrence reapplies the same f∘f∘f fixed-point operator; rotation gives each block a different residual-stream neighborhood per call, turning the 3 loop blocks into 3 distinct 3-block compositions (f∘g∘h at iter0, g∘h∘f at iter1, h∘f∘g at iter2) that partially specialize while sharing weights. Preserves exp_245's 1/sqrt(num_loops+1) ln_scale factor exactly (call counts unchanged). Zero byte risk: no new tensors, forward-pass order only.

hypothesis: Split Muon attention WD into sub-buckets by functional role: Q/K (post-proj RMSNormed → only direction matters) stay at muon_wd=0.095, V and attn.proj (content-routing path, structural analog of MLP's fc→proj) get muon_wd_attn_vp=0.115 matching current MLP WD.

Zero new params/bytes of consequence (~400B code delta). Novel axis vs exp_141 (depth-targeted recurrent ×1.3) and exp_164 (attn vs mlp type split). Worst case: attn_vp split is orthogonal to prior axes so a small move of 0.095→0.115 on just V+proj should be low-risk tuning. Rebased onto exp_336 snapshot first.

hypothesis: Replace per-shard random Latin-square phase permutation with a Van der Corput (bit-reversal) low-discrepancy visit order [0,4,2,6,1,5,3,7], rotated per-shard via random initial _phase_epoch offset: at 600s budget shards typically reset 2-3 times, so early-visit phase coverage dominates and random perms can unluckily draw adjacent-phase clusters that waste diversity; vdC guarantees maximal phase spread on every prefix for every shard.

Entropy-neutral: same K=8 bucket set, only the visit order changes → weight compression should track exp_336 (no size_blocked risk, unlike exp_326's K=16 entropy blow-up). Per-batch cross-shard diversity unchanged since initial offsets remain uniform. hypothesis: Freeze tied tok_emb.weight during TTT: the tied weight receives antagonistic gradients from both the input-embedding lookup and the output-projection path (F.linear against tok_emb.weight), destabilizing the ~120-step SGD adaptation; freezing isolates TTT updates to transformer blocks + scaling params.
notes: Single-site edit in eval_val_ttt: replace `ttt_params = [p for p in base_model.parameters()]` with a named-parameter filter excluding 'tok_emb', set requires_grad_(False) for all then True for ttt_params. Optimizer and clip_grad_norm already operate on ttt_params, so tok_emb is fully frozen. Concrete resurrection of aborted exp_049 (harness_abort, never completed). Motivation: with tie_embeddings=1 the vocab projection is F.linear(x, tok_emb.weight); TTT gradients to tok_emb arrive from both paths and typically oppose each other (pull embedding toward target vs push projection away from distractors). In the 4-epoch × ~30-batch SGD regime with cos_lr decay, this gradient interference is a likely loss-plateau mechanism. Zero size impact (only eval-path code). Risk: if tok_emb updates were the single largest positive contributor in baseline TTT, we lose signal — but previous direction-of-update tests (exp_039 mom 0.9→0.95 discard, exp_017 epochs 3→4 keep) suggest per-param noise reduction matters more than per-param aggressive updates.

hypothesis: Hessian-diag-weighted per-row sigma in GPTQ: replace unweighted W.std(dim=1) with sqrt(mean(W^2 * Hdiag/mean(Hdiag))), mean-rescaled to preserve layer-wide average scale. Aligns per-row quantization scale with the actual E[err^T H err] objective — rows with energy in high-activation columns get more clip headroom, low-H rows get finer resolution. Zero size cost.
notes: Single-site edit inside gptq_quantize_weight (after the ×1.02 diag damp, before permutation). Saves unpermuted Hdiag, constructs mean-1 column weights _Hwgt, replaces row_std=W.std with sqrt(weighted row-var) rescaled so layer-mean matches plain W.pow(2).mean(). Orthogonal to exp_348 (off-diag shrinkage, discard) and exp_339 (position-weighted calibration, discard): those modify H estimation; this modifies the scale chosen GIVEN H. Exp_280's ×1.02 damping is preserved. Complementary to exp_261's block_size=32 (kept, already in baseline? no — current baseline has block_size=64 default). Principled: under correlation between |W| and Hdiag (typical post-training), plain row_std under-weights scale on rows that most need clip headroom in loss-dominant columns.

hypothesis: Apply T=0.98 sub-unity logit temperature in both eval_val_sliding and eval_val_ttt score passes (divide logits by 0.98 before cross_entropy): opposite-direction probe vs exp_044's T=1.05 failure (+0.00261), motivated by GPTQ-induced logit-noise softening of the post-softmax distribution.
notes: Two-site edit: (1) eval_val_sliding line ~1289 and (2) eval_val_ttt score-pass line ~1368. Both change `logits.reshape(-1, logits.size(-1)).float()` to `(logits.reshape(-1, logits.size(-1)) / 0.98).float()`. TTT training pass (base_model(x,y)) is UNCHANGED — still CE at T=1 so TTT gradients remain true to the metric. Theory: GPTQ int6 injects ~1-2% logit noise; by Jensen's inequality E[softmax(x+ε)] is softer than softmax(E[x]), so sub-unity T recovers calibrated log-likelihood. Magnitude T=0.98 is conservative (exp_044's 1.05 was 5% softer and hurt +0.00261; 0.98 is 2% sharper, ~4x smaller perturbation). Compute delta: one FMA per logit, negligible vs model fwd. If this keeps, it opens a calibration sweep axis; if it fails symmetric to exp_044 it rules out the temperature axis decisively.

hypothesis: Concave (sqrt / u^0.5) position-weighted CE, mean-preserving 0.9 → 1.1 — probes the untested concave side of the shape-exponent family on the exp_336 baseline (linear, u^2 kept; u^4 discarded).
notes: Single-site edit in GPT.forward. Shape-exponent history on position-weighted CE (all mean-preserving 0.9→1.1): exp_156 linear kept Δ=-0.000200, exp_176 linear stack kept Δ=-0.000171, exp_186 u^2 kept Δ=-0.000062, exp_349 u^4 discard +0.000535, exp_034 wide-linear 0.5→1.5 harness_abort. Current exp_336 baseline DROPPED position weighting via rebase lineage 186→324→336, so this reintroduces weighting with a NEW shape: u^0.5 (concave). Compared to u^4 (discard), u^0.5 is the opposite side: sharp head downweight (pw≈0.871 at pos 0, vs 0.957 for u^4), gentle distributed tail upweight (pw≈1.065 at last pos, vs 1.170 for u^4). Tests whether the "first tokens no-context" signal dominates, not the "tail high-value" signal that u^4 tried to amplify. Zero new params, zero artifact bytes, ~1 linspace+sqrt+mean per forward (negligible compute).

hypothesis: Add a learnable per-feature embed_bias (shape=[512], init=0.0) applied POST-RMSNorm, as the additive complement to exp_298's gauge-fixed multiplicative embed_scale (inside-RMSNorm).
notes: Three-site edit. (1) __init__ L636: add self.embed_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(h.embedding_dim, dtype=torch.float32)) next to embed_scale. (2) forward_logits L743: add `x = x + self.embed_bias.to(x.dtype)` AFTER the F.rms_norm(x*embed_scale) line — applied post-norm so it survives RMSNorm (unlike embed_scale which sits inside).
